$comment: Controlled vocabulary for functional_claims[].type. Decision-oriented: types are grouped by the datasheet SECTION a designer consults and tagged with the part CLASSES they apply to. 'datasheet_slot' marks a type that fills an operational parameter an engineer selects/operates the part by (a structural prior for usefulness). 'aliases' grandfather every historical type string so existing data validates; new claims must use a canonical type. validate_parts.py enforces canonical-or-alias. Promote to a SKOS scheme at w3id.org/seqmake/claim-types once stable.

regulation_control.label: Regulation & control
regulation_control.purpose: How expression is switched/tuned: what induces or represses it and over what range.

performance.label: Performance
performance.purpose: Quantitative output: strength, efficiency, yield, copy number, sensitivity.

interaction_affinity.label: Interaction & affinity
interaction_affinity.purpose: What the part binds and how tightly: ligand, matrix, antibody, Kd.

processing_modification.label: Processing & modification
processing_modification.purpose: Cleavage/removal and post-translational changes the part undergoes or directs.

composition_structure.label: Composition & structure
composition_structure.purpose: What the part is built from and how it works: mechanism, domains, variants.

context_deployment.label: Context & deployment
context_deployment.purpose: Where/how it is used: host range, conditions, placement, applications.

selection_replication.label: Selection & replication
selection_replication.purpose: Replicon behaviour and selection: incompatibility, marker.

caveats_limitations.label: Caveats & limitations
caveats_limitations.purpose: Failure modes, interferences, context-dependence — risk-reducing knowledge.

identity_origin.label: Identity & origin
identity_origin.purpose: Provenance/definition. Low marginal usefulness unless it discriminates the part.

regulation.description: Overall control logic of a regulatory region (inducible/repressible/constitutive; the TF and signal).

inducer.description: The small-molecule/signal that activates or de-represses, ideally with effective concentration.

repressor.description: The repressor/activator protein acting on the part.

dynamic_range.description: Fold change between ON and OFF (induction or repression); value carries direction + fold.

induction_kinetics.description: Speed/shape of the ON or OFF transition.

orthogonality.description: Cross-talk/insulation versus other regulators or host machinery.

expression_yield.description: Accumulation level of product (e.g. % total cellular protein).

copy_number.description: Plasmid copies per cell and what sets it.

solubility.description: Effect on soluble yield of a fusion passenger.

binding_affinity.description: Quantitative binding strength (Kd/Ka) to a partner.

ligand.description: Native ligand/substrate underlying a binding handle.

affinity_matrix.description: Purification matrix the part binds and the elution condition.

antibody_recognition.description: Antibodies that recognise the epitope and their requirements.

tag_removal.description: Protease and site used to remove the tag/cleave the fusion.

recognition_specificity.description: Sequence specificity of an enzyme acting on the part (cleavage/recombination).

post_translational.description: PTMs the part can undergo (sulfation, phosphorylation, ...) and their consequence.

mechanism.description: How the part works at the molecular level.

domain_function.description: Function attributable to a specific domain/region.

structure.description: Domain architecture / fold relevant to use.

biophysics.description: Oligomeric state, stability, and similar biophysical properties.

sequence_variant.description: Named variants/mutants and minimal motifs, and how they differ from this part.

sequence_feature.description: A mapped sub-feature (TSS, spacer, dyad) not captured as a structured feature.

host_range.description: Organisms/contexts the part is validated or expected to work in.

application.description: A concrete use the part enables (e.g. dSTORM imaging, two-step purification).

placement.description: N-/C-terminal or positional constraints on use.

caveat.description: Failure mode, interference, context-dependence, or scar a user must plan around.

origin.description: Provenance/derivation of the sequence. Useful only when it discriminates the part.

function.description: GENERIC fallback. Discouraged — re-type to a specific claim_type; a bare 'function' claim is usually low-usefulness or mis-typed.
